messages.content: T. Dang, T. Hoang, and T. N. Nguyen, "Open-Circuit Fault Detection in Three-Phase Inverter-Fed Induction Motors Using a Model-Based Method," IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications, vol. 56, no. 1, pp. 146-156, 2020.

The article titled "Open-Circuit Fault Detection in Three-Phase Inverter-Fed Induction Motors Using a Model-Based Method" was written by T. Dang, T. Hoang, and T. N. Nguyen. It was published in the IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications, Volume 56, Issue 1, in 2020. The article focuses on employing a model-based method to detect open-circuit faults in three-phase inverter-fed induction motors. The authors present a comprehensive analysis of the method and its effectiveness in detecting such faults. The article spans from page 146 to 156 in the journal.

X. Wei, Z. Wu, Y. Lin, and B. Xu, "A Comprehensive Detection Method for Open-Circuit Faults in Three-Phase Inverter-Fed Induction Motors Based on Phase Currents and Zero-Sequence Voltage," IEEE Access, vol. 7, pp. 130510-130520, 2019.

The article titled "A Comprehensive Detection Method for Open-Circuit Faults in Three-Phase Inverter-Fed Induction Motors Based on Phase Currents and Zero-Sequence Voltage" was written by X. Wei, Z. Wu, Y. Lin, and B. Xu. It was published in the IEEE Access journal, Volume 7, in 2019. The article proposes a detection method for open-circuit faults in three-phase inverter-fed induction motors, utilizing both phase currents and zero-sequence voltage. The authors provide a thorough analysis of the method and its effectiveness in detecting and diagnosing such faults. The article spans from page 130510 to 130520 in the journal.

A. Khodayari, A. Abbaspour-Tamijani, and M. Mirsalim, "Online detection of open-circuit fault in three-phase inverter-fed induction motors using deep belief networks," ISA Transactions, vol. 93, pp. 254-263, 2019

The article titled "Online detection of open-circuit fault in three-phase inverter-fed induction motors using deep belief networks" was written by A. Khodayari, A. Abbaspour-Tamijani, and M. Mirsalim. It was published in the ISA Transactions journal, Volume 93, in 2019. The article proposes an online detection method for open-circuit faults in three-phase inverter-fed induction motors using deep belief networks. The authors explore the effectiveness of this approach and present their findings in detecting such faults. The article spans from page 254 to 263 in the journal.

C. Zhang, Y. Feng, Q. Han, and Y. Lin, "An Open-Circuit Fault Diagnosis Method for Three-Phase Inverter-Fed Induction Motors Based on Improved EEMD and ELM," IEEE Access, vol. 7, pp. 167855-167863, 2019

The article titled "An Open-Circuit Fault Diagnosis Method for Three-Phase Inverter-Fed Induction Motors Based on Improved EEMD and ELM" was written by C. Zhang, Y. Feng, Q. Han, and Y. Lin. It was published in the IEEE Access journal, Volume 7, in 2019. The article presents a fault diagnosis method for open-circuit faults in three-phase inverter-fed induction motors. The proposed method incorporates improved ensemble empirical mode decomposition (EEMD) and extreme learning machine (ELM) techniques. The authors provide a thorough explanation of the method and assess its effectiveness in diagnosing such faults. The article spans from page 167855 to 167863 in the journal.

Fault Detection and Localisation of a Three-Phase Inverter with Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor Load Using a Convolutional Neural Network
Łuczak, Dominik; Brock, Stefan; Siembab, Krzysztof.  Actuators; Basel Vol. 12, Iss. 3,  (2023)

The article titled "Fault Detection and Localization of a Three-Phase Inverter with Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor Load Using a Convolutional Neural Network" was written by Dominik Łuczak, Stefan Brock, and Krzysztof Siembab. It was published in the Actuators journal, Volume 12, Issue 3, in 2023. The article focuses on the detection and localization of faults in a three-phase inverter with a permanent magnet synchronous motor load. The authors propose the use of a convolutional neural network (CNN) for fault detection and localization. The article provides a detailed explanation of the methodology and its effectiveness in detecting and localizing faults. The article spans from page 1 to 18 in the journal.

The article titled "Inter-turn short circuit fault analysis of PWM inverter fed three-phase induction motor using Finite Element Method" was written by K. Prasob, N. Praveen Kumar, and T. B. Isha. It was presented at the 2017 International Conference on Circuit, Power and Computing Technologies (ICCPCT). The article focuses on the analysis of inter-turn short circuit faults in a three-phase induction motor fed by a PWM inverter. The authors utilize the Finite Element Method (FEM) to analyze the effects of such faults on the motor performance. The article provides detailed insights into the fault analysis methodology and its findings.

The article titled "Inter-turn Stator Winding Short Circuit Fault Analysis in Inverter fed Induction Motor using FEM" was written by M. Karthic Krishna, K. Kishan Kumar, M. Koushik, K. Sudhakaran, and N. Praveen Kumar. It was presented at the 2018 3rd IEEE International Conference on Recent Trends in Electronics, Information & Communication Technology (RTEICT). The article focuses on the analysis of inter-turn stator winding short circuit faults in an induction motor fed by an inverter using the Finite Element Method (FEM). The authors explore the effects of these faults on the motor's performance and provide insights into the fault analysis using FEM. The article presents the findings and observations from the analysis.

B. Wang, J. Wang, A. Griffo and W. Hua, "Effective Turn Fault Mitigation by Creating Zero Sequence Current Path for a Triple Redundant 3 × 3-Phase PMA SynRM," in IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics, vol. 34, no. 11, pp. 11080-11089, Nov. 2019, doi: 10.1109/TPEL.2019.2900441.

The article titled "Effective Turn Fault Mitigation by Creating Zero Sequence Current Path for a Triple Redundant 3 × 3-Phase PMA SynRM" was written by B. Wang, J. Wang, A. Griffo, and W. Hua. It was published in the IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics journal, Volume 34, Issue 11, in November 2019. The article focuses on the effective mitigation of turn faults in a triple redundant 3 × 3-phase Permanent Magnet Assisted Synchronous Reluctance Motor (PMA SynRM) by creating a zero sequence current path. The authors propose a fault mitigation strategy and investigate its effectiveness. The article provides detailed analysis, experimental results, and discussion on the implementation of the proposed method. The article spans from page 11080 to 11089 and can be accessed with the doi: 10.1109/TPEL.2019.2900441.

Syafii, H. Yamashika and Adrianti, "Characteristics of Symmetrical Components During Unsymmetrical Faults in Distribution Network with PV-Inverter Connected," 2023 International Conference for Advancement in Technology (ICONAT), Goa, India, 2023, pp. 1-5, doi: 10.1109/ICONAT57137.2023.10080061.

The article titled "Characteristics of Symmetrical Components During Unsymmetrical Faults in Distribution Network with PV-Inverter Connected" was written by H. Syafii, H. Yamashika, and Adrianti. It was presented at the 2023 International Conference for Advancement in Technology (ICONAT) in Goa, India. The article explores the characteristics of symmetrical components during unsymmetrical faults in a distribution network that is connected to a PV inverter. The authors investigate the behavior of symmetrical components and analyze their impact on the network during unsymmetrical fault conditions. The article provides detailed insights, experimental results, and analysis of the symmetrical components' characteristics. The article spans from page 1 to 5 and can be accessed with the doi: 10.1109/ICONAT57137.2023.10080061.

B. Wang, J. Wang, A. Griffo, Z. Sun and E. Chong, "A fault tolerant machine drive based on permanent magnet assisted synchronous reluctance machine," 2016 IEEE Energy Conversion Congress and Exposition (ECCE), Milwaukee, WI, USA, 2016, pp. 1-8, doi: 10.1109/ECCE.2016.7854677.

The article titled "A fault tolerant machine drive based on permanent magnet assisted synchronous reluctance machine" was written by B. Wang, J. Wang, A. Griffo, Z. Sun, and E. Chong. It was presented at the 2016 IEEE Energy Conversion Congress and Exposition (ECCE) in Milwaukee, WI, USA. The article introduces a fault tolerant machine drive system based on a permanent magnet assisted synchronous reluctance machine. The authors propose a fault detection and mitigation strategy for the drive system, emphasizing the use of permanent magnets to enhance fault tolerance. The article provides detailed analysis, experimental results, and discussion on the performance and effectiveness of the proposed system. The article spans from page 1 to 8 and can be accessed with the doi: 10.1109/ECCE.2016.7854677.
